page: Amazon Web Services m8in.96xlarge is a general-purpose, network- and EBS-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel processors with 192 physical cores and 384 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) via 2.0 threads per core. The dedicated CPUs operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz.

The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options such as Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS), for which the instance is optimized.

Networking capabilities include a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. This high network throughput, combined with the massive compute and memory resources, makes the m8in.96xlarge suitable for large-scale general-purpose workloads, network-intensive applications, enterprise databases, and distributed containerized environments.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.96xlarge is a general-purpose, network- and EBS-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It is powered by Intel x86_64 processors with 192 physical cores and 384 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.9 GHz. The instance features 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage. With a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, this instance is designed for high-throughput networking and resource-intensive enterprise applications. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, distributed container deployments, and network-heavy workloads requiring substantial compute capacity.

tagline: A high-capacity, network-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel processors, massive memory, and 300 Gbps baseline bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
